steps.title: Find the axis (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a single vertical line for the center of the lantern. Everything will balance around this axis.

steps.title: Block the glass body (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, sketch a tall rounded rectangle centered on the axis for the glass chamber — taller than it is wide, with softly rounded corners.

steps.title: Block the cap and base (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a wider trapezoid above the body for the vented cap — narrower at the top. Add a matching trapezoid below, narrower at the bottom, for the base. Two short horizontal lines mark where they meet the body.

steps.title: Plan the handle (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch a curved arc rising from the top of the cap and looping back down. A simple semicircle works. Keep it light — it'll meet the cap at two clean points.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the body and cap
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the outline of the cap, body, and base in one mostly continuous pass. Where the cap meets the body, land your lines right at the junction — no overshoot.

steps.title: Ink the handle (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, trace the curved handle in one confident stroke. Where it meets the cap, the two lines should touch cleanly without crossing.

steps.title: Ink the inner details (ink)
steps.description: Add a small candle or wick shape inside the glass — a short vertical line with a teardrop flame on top. A few short horizontal lines in the cap suggest the vent slots.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove the axis line and pencil guides. The lantern should feel sturdy enough to swing from a hook.
